description: Fix critical and high-severity documentation accuracy errors: wrong CLI flags, wrong file paths, wrong URLs, optional fields shown as required, and missing API documentation.
qualityChecks: cd docs && bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip

userStories.title: Fix CLI reference: logs flags, config show --json, plugin create args, doctor checks
userStories.description: As a user reading the CLI reference, I want accurate flag and argument documentation so I don't use non-existent flags or expect wrong behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: The `opentabs logs` section documents `-f, --follow` as an opt-in flag (not `--no-follow`). Default behavior is described as printing recent lines and exiting, with `--follow` enabling tailing.
The `config show --json` description says it outputs the config with the secret redacted (not 'includes the actual secret value')
The `plugin create` section shows `<name>` and `--domain` as optional (not required), and mentions interactive mode when no arguments are provided
The `doctor` checks list includes all actual checks from the source: MCP client config check and npm plugin health check are added (9 total checks)
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx. Four fixes needed:

1. Around line 268: Replace `--no-follow` flag documentation with `-f, --follow`. The actual CLI (platform/cli/src/commands/logs.ts:179) uses `.option('-f, --follow', 'Follow new output (like tail -f)')`. Default is print-and-exit. Also fix the description around line 280 that says following is the default.

2. Around line 519: Change `--json` description from 'includes the actual secret value' to 'outputs the config with the secret redacted'. The actual code (platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts:47-50) calls `redactSecret(config)` before JSON output. Also fix the example around line 529 if it shows `jq -r .secret` as if it returns a real secret.

3. Around line 342: Change `<name>` from 'Required: Yes' to optional. Change `--domain` from 'Required: Yes' to optional. Add note about interactive mode.

4. Around lines 226-234: Add the 2 missing doctor checks (MCP client config, npm plugin health) so the list has all 9 checks from platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts.

userStories.title: Fix first-plugin.mdx scaffold directory name and contributing docs
userStories.description: As a plugin developer following the tutorial, I want the correct directory name after scaffolding so I don't get confused when `cd opentabs-plugin-my-app` fails.
acceptanceCriteria: first-plugin.mdx line 20 says `cd my-app` instead of `cd opentabs-plugin-my-app`
The scaffold command and cd are consistent across all docs pages that reference scaffolding (first-plugin.mdx, install/index.mdx, guides/plugin-development.mdx)
contributing/publishing.mdx references `scripts/publish.ts` (not `publish.sh`)
contributing/publishing.mdx lists all 5 published packages including `create-plugin` and shows the correct publish order
contributing/publishing.mdx correctly states `create-plugin` is part of the `@opentabs-dev` scope
contributing/dev-setup.mdx line 19 uses `https://github.com/opentabs-dev/opentabs.git` (not AnomalyCo)
contributing/dev-setup.mdx line 11 says Bun v1.3.9+ (not v1.1+)
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes:
Multiple files need fixes:

1. docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx line 20: Change `cd opentabs-plugin-my-app` to `cd my-app`. The scaffold function (platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts:470) creates directory named `args.name`, not `opentabs-plugin-${args.name}`.

2. docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx around line 92: Uses `npx create-opentabs-plugin my-plugin` — change to `bunx create-opentabs-plugin my-plugin` per the Bun-first convention.

3. docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx:
   - Line 69: Change `scripts/publish.sh` to `scripts/publish.ts`. Invocation is `bun scripts/publish.ts 0.0.17`.
   - Lines 10-43: Add `create-plugin` to the package list (5 packages total: shared, plugin-sdk, plugin-tools, cli, create-plugin).
   - Line 82: Update dependency order to include create-plugin.
   - Line 113: Remove incorrect claim about create-plugin being outside @opentabs-dev scope.
   - Lines 78-82: Fix the build step description to match actual script behavior (verify auth -> bump versions -> update cross-refs -> rebuild -> publish).

4. docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx:
   - Line 19: Replace `AnomalyCo` with `opentabs-dev` in git clone URL.
   - Line 11: Replace `v1.1+` with `v1.3.9+` per root package.json engines field.

userStories.title: Fix SDK reference: optional fields shown as required, missing code params
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the SDK reference, I want accurate type information so I use the API correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: streaming-progress.mdx shows `progress` and `total` as optional fields in the ProgressOptions interface and table
sdk/tools.mdx shows `displayName` and `icon` as optional in the ToolDefinition properties table
sdk/error-types.mdx documents the optional `code` parameter on all 6 factory methods (auth, notFound, rateLimited, validation, timeout, internal)
guides/error-handling.mdx factory method signatures also include the optional `code` parameter
sdk/utilities.mdx shows the correct error codes: `TIMEOUT` (uppercase) for fetchFromPage and `VALIDATION_ERROR` for fetchJSON parse failure
sdk/utilities.mdx documents the `waitForSelector` generic type parameter `<T extends Element = Element>`
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes:
Multiple SDK reference files need fixes:

1. docs/content/docs/guides/streaming-progress.mdx lines 75-83: Change `progress: number` and `total: number` to `progress?: number` and `total?: number`. Update the table (lines 96-106) to show both as 'No' for Required. Add JSDoc note: 'Omit for indeterminate progress.'

2. docs/content/docs/sdk/tools.mdx: Find the ToolDefinition properties table. Change `displayName` from required to optional (actual: `displayName?: string` at platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts:168). Change `icon` from required to optional (actual: `icon?: LucideIconName` at index.ts:172).

3. docs/content/docs/sdk/error-types.mdx lines 130-197: All 6 factory methods need the optional `code` parameter added:
   - `ToolError.auth(message, code?)` — not just `auth(message)`
   - `ToolError.notFound(message, code?)` — already correct
   - `ToolError.rateLimited(message, retryAfterMs?, code?)` — add code
   - `ToolError.validation(message, code?)` — add code
   - `ToolError.timeout(message, code?)` — add code
   - `ToolError.internal(message, code?)` — add code

4. docs/content/docs/guides/error-handling.mdx lines 54, 135, 180, 218, 263: Same factory method signature fixes as above.

5. docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx:
   - Line 198: Change code `'timeout'` to `'TIMEOUT'`
   - Line 227: Change code `'json_parse_error'` to `'VALIDATION_ERROR'`
   - Line 34: Change `Promise<Element>` to `Promise<T>` and document the generic parameter

userStories.title: Document missing SDK exports: postForm, postFormData, Zod schema overloads
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want complete documentation of all SDK exports so I can discover and use all available utilities.
acceptanceCriteria: sdk/utilities.mdx documents `postForm(url, body, init?)` with its signature, description, and a code example
sdk/utilities.mdx documents `postFormData(url, body, init?)` with its signature, description, and a code example

sdk/utilities.mdx documents the Zod schema validation overloads for `fetchJSON`, `postJSON`, `putJSON`, `patchJSON`, and `deleteJSON` — showing how to pass a Zod schema for type-safe response validation
sdk/prompts.mdx documents the `args` field on PromptDefinition and the typed `definePrompt` overload
sdk/resources.mdx documents the `schema` property on ResourceDefinition
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes:
New documentation sections needed:

1. docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx — Add after the existing postJSON section:
   - `postForm<T>(url, body, init?)` — exported from plugin-sdk (index.ts:300, fetch.ts:197-214). POST with `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` body. `body` is `Record<string, string>`, encoded with URLSearchParams.
   - `postFormData<T>(url, body, init?)` — exported from plugin-sdk (index.ts:301, fetch.ts:238-255). POST with `multipart/form-data` body. `body` is a FormData instance.
   - Zod schema overloads: `fetchJSON<T>(url, init, schema)` and `postJSON<T>(url, body, init, schema)` accept a Zod schema as the final argument. Response is validated against the schema, throwing ToolError.validation on mismatch. Same for `putJSON`, `patchJSON`, `deleteJSON`.

2. docs/content/docs/sdk/prompts.mdx around line 21: Add `args?: z.ZodObject<z.ZodRawShape>` to the PromptDefinition interface. Document the typed `definePrompt` overload that provides type-safe render args.

3. docs/content/docs/sdk/resources.mdx around line 27: Add `schema?: TContent` to ResourceDefinition. Explain it enables typed resource content.

userStories.title: Fix reference docs: troubleshooting auth, MCP server health fields, override keyword
userStories.description: As a user debugging issues, I want accurate troubleshooting guidance and complete API reference.
acceptanceCriteria: troubleshooting.mdx correctly describes how the extension gets its auth secret (reads from auth.json, not via /ws-info)
troubleshooting.mdx notes that unauthenticated /health returns minimal fields only
reference/mcp-server.mdx health endpoint response example includes the missing fields: discoveryErrors, lastReloadTimestamp, lastReloadDurationMs, stateSchemaVersion, fileWatcher
reference/mcp-server.mdx has a dedicated section for the /ws-info endpoint response format
Code examples in first-plugin.mdx and guides/plugin-development.mdx do not use `override` on abstract members (use plain `readonly` instead)
`cd docs && bun run build` passes
`cd docs && bun run type-check` passes
`cd docs && bun run lint` passes
`cd docs && bun run knip` passes
userStories.notes:
1. docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx line 31: Change 'fetches the auth secret via /ws-info' to 'reads the auth secret from auth.json (written to ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json by the server on startup)'. The /ws-info endpoint itself requires Bearer auth.

2. docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx around line 102: Add note that `curl http://localhost:9515/health` without Bearer auth returns only minimal fields (status, version, extensionConnected).

3. docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx lines 148-173: Add missing fields to the health response example JSON: `discoveryErrors` (array), `lastReloadTimestamp` (ISO string), `lastReloadDurationMs` (number), `stateSchemaVersion` (number), `fileWatcher` (object with sub-fields). Check platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts for the actual response shape.

4. docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx around line 34: Add a ### /ws-info section after the endpoints table with the response format.

5. docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx line 63 and docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx line 75: Remove `override` keyword from abstract property implementations. `displayName` is abstract on the base class, so `readonly displayName = 'My App'` is correct (not `override readonly displayName`).
